dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8920 IH: Restoring the American Homebuyers Dream Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Biggs of Arizona (for himself, Mr. Moore of Alabama, and Mr. Donalds) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to direct the Secretary of the Treasury to share Individual Taxpayer Identification Number information with the Department of Homeland Security for the purpose of enforcing immigration laws.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Restoring the American Homebuyers Dream Act.

paragraph.text: Unchecked illegal immigration under the Biden Administration created a crisis in the housing market for American families.
There is a direct correlation between increased housing costs and an influx of illegal aliens.
Areas with the highest rates of illegal immigration have seen double digit increases in housing costs.
Illegal aliens have used Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers to secure housing loans, putting them in direct competition with American families for housing.

section.header: Disclosure of ITIN information to Department of Homeland Security

subsection.text: Section 6103 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end of subsection (l) the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Disclosure of individual taxpayer identification number information for immigrant enforcement

subparagraph.text: The Secretary shall, upon written request by the Secretary of Homeland Security for the purpose of enforcing United States immigration laws, disclose the following information with respect to any individual issued an individual taxpayer identification number (ITIN) under section 6109:

clause.text: The name, address, and ITIN of the individual.
The filing status of the individual.
The name and ITIN of any dependent claimed if the dependent is over 18 years of age, if applicable.
The name and ITIN of a claimed spouse, if applicable.
Such other identifying information as the Secretary determines is reasonably necessary to verify identity or immigration status.

subparagraph.text: Any information disclosed under this paragraph shall be used solely for the purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, enforcement of applicable immigration laws, as determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security, and shall be protected by all safeguards and penalties that apply to taxpayer information under this section.

subsection.text: The amendment made by this section shall apply to disclosures after the date of the enactment of this Act.
